title: Seagrass Overgrowth
description: Seagrass Overgrowth brings the ocean to life by replacing plain seagrass with overgrown aquatic remnants.
body:
## 🌊 Seagrass Overgrowth
The ocean floor shouldn’t feel empty—it should feel alive.

Seagrass Overgrowth enhances underwater biomes by replacing plain seagrass with dense, natural overgrowth that blends seamlessly with coral reefs and the surrounding seafloor. Seagrass adopts more neutral, balanced tones, allowing it to visually adapt to nearby coral and underwater terrain for a cohesive, immersive look.

To add more life and surprises, the pack also scatters random seashells, baby nautiluses, starfish, hidden treasures, and fun Easter eggs throughout the underwater landscape.

This pack is designed to feel natural, not modded—perfect for survival worlds, vanilla+ packs, and performance-focused setups.
## ✨ Features
- Multiple visual variants for seagrass
- Denser, more natural underwater growth
- Neutral color tones that blend with coral and seabed blocks
- Vanilla-friendly and lightweight
- No gameplay changes—purely visual enhancement

## 🎯 Perfect For
- Vanilla+ modpacks
- Desert exploration builds
- Players who want more atmosphere without clutter

Make the oceans feel alive—by letting them grow naturally.

## 🧩 Dependencies
- No dependencies.

## 📝 Note
- If you want to use shaders and remove the swaying animation on seagrass, open the .zip file of your shader pack, navigate to shaders/block.properties, and delete the line containing minecraft: seagrass.

---
